The `-a` and `-r` options make changes to the system's plug-in registry. The registry is
designed to operate automatically, and will update on its own as

executive_summary.description: A high-level summary addressing the core problem. It explains that the persistent `pluginkit` tombstone is likely not in a separate `pkd` database but within the persistent LaunchServices database (`csstore`), and that `pluginkit -r` is not designed to be irreversible. The recommended solution involves rebuilding this database.

It is a persistent marker left after using `pluginkit -r` that incorrectly prevents the system from re-registering the same plugin, even though this behavior is not the documented intent of the command.
